title: Dendranthema × rubellum
taxonomy: Dendranthema × rubellum is classified as a hybrid species within the family Asteraceae, specifically within the tribe Anthemideae. This classification is based on the plant's botanical characteristics and its evolutionary relationships with other species in the same family. Dendranthema × rubellum is a cross between Dendranthema grandiflorum and Dendranthema rubellum. The exact classification may vary depending on the source and the current understanding of plant taxonomy. In general, Dendranthema × rubellum is classified as a type of chrysanthemum, which is a flowering plant commonly used in gardens and as cut flowers.
uses: Dendranthema × rubellum, also known as the rubellum chrysanthemum, has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's extracts have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial activity, which can help treat various infections and wounds. In some cultures, Dendranthema × rubellum is used to treat respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds, due to its expectorant properties. Additionally, the plant's antiseptic properties make it useful for treating skin conditions, such as acne and dermatitis. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses and potential benefits of Dendranthema × rubellum.
benefits: Dendranthema × rubellum, also known as the rose chrysanthemum, has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of arthritis and other inflammatory conditions. Additionally, it is believed to have antioxidant properties, which can help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. Some studies suggest that Dendranthema × rubellum may also have antimicrobial and antiviral properties, making it useful in the treatment of infections. More research is needed to fully understand the health benefits of this plant.
properties: Dendranthema × rubellum, also known as the Ruby Mound Chrysanthemum, is a plant with potential therapeutic properties. Research has shown that this plant may possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which could be beneficial for wound healing and skin care. The plant's extracts may also have antioxidant effects, helping to protect cells from damage caused by free radicals. Furthermore, the Ruby Mound Chrysanthemum may have antiseptic and antifungal properties, making it a promising candidate for natural remedies. However, more studies are needed to fully understand the therapeutic potential of Dendranthema × rubellum.
constituents: Dendranthema × rubellum, also known as the Mountain Cheddar or Mountain Pink Cheddar, contains a range of active constituents, including sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids. These compounds are responsible for its medicinal and potential therapeutic properties. The specific constituents may vary depending on factors such as the plant's growing conditions and geographical location. parts: Dendranthema × rubellum, also known as Ruby Moor Chrysanthemum, has been used in traditional medicine, particularly in the treatment of various gastrointestinal issues. The plant's roots and leaves are considered medicinal parts. However, more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and potential side effects. In traditional medicine, it's often used to treat diarrhea, fever, and other digestive problems. preparations: Dendranthema × rubellum is commonly known as the Bedstraw Chrysanthemum, and its herbal preparations are used in traditional medicine. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are utilized in various herbal remedies. In traditional herbal medicine, Dendranthema × rubellum is used to treat wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. Its herbal preparations are often used in the form of teas, tinctures, and topical creams. Further research is needed to fully understand the efficacy and potential benefits of using Dendranthema × rubellum in herbal preparations.
side_effects: Dendranthema × rubellum, also known as the Chrysanthemum 'Sheffield Pink', is a plant that does not appear to have significant side effects when handled or inhaled as long as proper precautions are taken. However, like many other plants in the chrysanthemum family, it can cause skin irritation or allergic reactions in some individuals. Ingesting any part of the plant can be toxic and may lead to symptoms such as vomiting, diarrhea, or abdominal pain. If you plan to grow or handle Dendranthema × rubellum, it's essential to wear protective gloves and clothing. It's also crucial to keep the plant out of reach of children and pets to prevent any potential harm.
